content.problemStatement:
You are given a sequence A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã and you have to perform the following operation exactly XXX times:


Choose two integers iii and jjj such that 1‚â§i<j‚â§N1 \le i \lt j \le N1‚â§i<j‚â§N.
Choose a non-negative integer ppp.
Change AiA_iAi‚Äã to Ai‚äï2pA_i \oplus 2^pAi‚Äã‚äï2p, and change AjA_jAj‚Äã to Aj‚äï2pA_j \oplus 2^pAj‚Äã‚äï2p, where ‚äï\oplus‚äï denotes bitwise XOR.
Find the lexicographically smallest sequence which can be obtained by performing this operation exactly XXX times.

A sequence B1,B2,‚Ä¶,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1‚Äã,B2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,BN‚Äã is said to be lexicographically smaller than a sequence C1,C2,‚Ä¶,CNC_1, C_2, \ldots, C_NC1‚Äã,C2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,CN‚Äã if there is an index iii such that Bi<CiB_i \lt C_iBi‚Äã<Ci‚Äã and for each valid j<ij \lt ij<i, Bj=CjB_j = C_jBj‚Äã=Cj‚Äã.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and XXX.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing NNN space-separated integers ‚Äï the lexicographically smallest obtainable sequence.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: The original sequence is (2,2,3)(2, 2, 3)(2,2,3). Consider the following three operations:

Choose i=1i = 1i=1, j=2j = 2j=2 and p=1p = 1p=1. Then, A1A_1A1‚Äã changes from 222 to 2‚äï21=02 \oplus 2^1 = 02‚äï21=0 and similarly, A2A_2A2‚Äã changes from 222 to 2‚äï21=02 \oplus 2^1 = 02‚äï21=0. Now the sequence is (0,0,3)(0, 0, 3)(0,0,3).
Next, choose i=1i = 1i=1, j=2j = 2j=2 and p=1p = 1p=1. Then, A1A_1A1‚Äã changes to 0‚äï21=20 \oplus 2^1 = 20‚äï21=2 and A2A_2A2‚Äã changes to 0‚äï21=20 \oplus 2^1 = 20‚äï21=2. The sequence is (2,2,3)(2, 2, 3)(2,2,3).
Next, again choose i=1i = 1i=1, j=2j = 2j=2 and p=1p = 1p=1. Then, A1A_1A1‚Äã changes to 2‚äï21=02 \oplus 2^1 = 02‚äï21=0 and A2A_2A2‚Äã changes to 2‚äï21=02 \oplus 2^1 = 02‚äï21=0. The sequence is (0,0,3)(0, 0, 3)(0,0,3) again.

We can check that after exactly 3 operations, this is the lexicographically smallest sequence we can obtain.
